name: Ecosystem Module Index (PR-F2, handover F-002)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Feature 031 (NIP-0001 acceptance + phase-005 plan of record) is committed at HEAD (45e3b67). This feature (handover F-002) completes the existing 11-line ecosystem/README.md stub into the canonical module index: it must define the full 10-stage lifecycle (Bootstrap->Preflight->Baseline->Audit->Plan->Execute->Verify->Promote->Compare->Repeat, per product_spec_005.md Sec 1) and navigate the 9 numbered protocol documents in ecosystem/ (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md .. 08_ga_gate.md, per docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md 'Proposed Framework Surface'), and index the ecosystem module in NIZAM.json's modules array. Only ecosystem/README.md and NIZAM.json are edited by this feature.
design_notes.design_constraint_no_dangling_references: Of the 9 numbered protocol documents named in NIP-0001's module layout, only ecosystem/README.md exists on disk as of this contract. 033 (dep 031, running CONCURRENTLY with this feature in the same working tree) will add ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md; 034/035/036 (each downstream of 033/034, NOT started yet) will add 02_evidence_baseline.md/03_engineering_audit.md/07_progress_comparison.md; 00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 04_dependency_reconciliation.md, 05_release_train_coordination.md, 06_simplification_review.md, and 08_ga_gate.md are DEFERRABLE beyond phase 005 entirely per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3 and may never land in this phase. Empirically, tools/validate.sh's C9 (repo-wide path resolution) and C4 (NIZAM.json indexed-path walker) do NOT currently sweep the ecosystem/ directory's body text at all (confirmed by reading tools/validate.sh's build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set -- ecosystem/ is absent from both, and by `grep -n ecosystem tools/validate.sh` returning no match) -- so this design constraint is NOT enforced by the shipped validator today. It is enforced proactively by this contract for two independent reasons: (1) a future validator extension (feature 042, 'Extend validator + CI fixtures') is very likely to add ecosystem/ to the shipped-doc set, and this feature must not create dangling references that would break that migration; (2) NIZAM.json's modules[].key_documents IS already walked unconditionally by the existing, shipped C4 check, so listing a not-yet-existing protocol doc there would break C4 TODAY, not hypothetically. Resolution: (a) NIZAM.json's new 'ecosystem' module entry's key_documents is EXACTLY the single-element list ['ecosystem/README.md'] (the sole currently-shipped doc), enforced by exact-list equality in verification[13]/[14] (REVISED per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json required_fix #1 -- a membership-only test would tolerate a second, out-of-contract key_document, including a dangling one, or one that stops being dangling the moment feature 033 lands ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md in this same concurrently-worked tree) -- future features (033-036, or 040's capability-registration feature) extend key_documents incrementally, each under its own contract, as each protocol doc lands; (b) ecosystem/README.md's module-navigation table names the 9 protocol documents by their BARE filename (e.g. '01_clean_state_preflight.md'), never in the directory-qualified 'ecosystem/NN_....md' form that tools/validate.sh's C9 regex (`[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_./-]*\.(md|json|sh|html|yml)`) would extract as a path-reference candidate, annotating each as Shipped or Planned (dynamically checked against the real filesystem by verification[9]/[10] below, per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json's unsupported-claims finding); verification[11] below is a regression guard proving zero C9-shaped directory-qualified tokens in the README body fail to resolve on disk (0 tokens today; remains true as long as the bare-filename convention is followed by this and future features).
design_notes.known_false_pass_in_official_acceptance_: The feature_list_005.json-specified acceptance-test command for 'NIZAM.json indexes the ecosystem module' is `grep -q 'ecosystem' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0`. Dry-run proof: this ALREADY EXITS 0 at HEAD (before any 032 edit), because NIZAM.json already contains unrelated pre-existing case-sensitive substring matches for 'ecosystem' (registry/README.md's purpose text 'ecosystem-level registries', standard/cross_repo_governance.md's summary 'ecosystem-scale governance', methodology/08's summary 'ecosystem dependency graph') -- confirmed via `git show HEAD:NIZAM.json | grep -n ecosystem`, 3 hits, all pre-existing prose, zero module entries. This is the exact class of defect the evaluator rejected contract 031 rev0 for (a bare substring match already true at HEAD). Per that precedent, this contract KEEPS the literal official command (verification[12], for spec traceability) but does NOT treat it as the real gate: verification[13]/[14] below are the actual, structurally-sound, anti-vacuity-proven check (requires an actual `{"path": "ecosystem", ...}` object in the modules array with a non-empty purpose and key_documents EQUAL TO the exact single-element list ['ecosystem/README.md'] -- REVISED to exact-list equality per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json required_fix #1, a membership test was insufficient) and are the blocking requirement.
design_notes.c8_and_c7_do_not_apply: ecosystem/README.md is outside build_shipped_md_set (confirmed by reading tools/validate.sh), so it is NOT part of the default C1/C2/C3/C5/C8/C9/C10 sweep and C8 (version-bump-vs-changelog) does not enforce a version bump on it. This contract nonetheless REQUIRES the frontmatter version to be a genuine semver increase past the existing stub's 0.1.0 (REVISED per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json required_fix #2 from a mere not-equal test, which a 0.0.1 downgrade would have passed, to a real MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH tuple comparison strictly greater than (0,1,0)) with a matching change_log entry (verification[5]/[6]), for NDS consistency with every other governed document in this repository, even though tools/validate.sh will not itself enforce it. C7 (module-README presence) is a fixed list (standard/methodology/registry/templates/schema/tools) that does not include ecosystem/ and this feature does not add it there -- out of scope (non_goals).
design_notes.revision1_corrections: REVISION 1 of 3 (per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=0, REJECTED). Three findings, two fixed here as blocking, one closed by adding coverage; nothing else changed. FIX 1 (HIGH, blocking, verification_index 11 in the reviewed rev0 numbering -- now verification[13]): the structural NIZAM.json module-entry check's acceptance_test TEXT claimed key_documents 'containing exactly' 'ecosystem/README.md' but the command only tested set MEMBERSHIP (`'ecosystem/README.md' in key_documents`). The evaluator built a scratch NIZAM.json copy (never touching the real, contracted NIZAM.json) with key_documents=['ecosystem/README.md','ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md'] (the second path not existing on disk at review time) and proved the membership check PASSED despite the extra, out-of-contract, dangling key_document -- and further proved that once feature 033 lands that second path in this same concurrently-worked tree (simulated via a reverted touch), BOTH the membership check AND the incidental C4 backstop pass, leaving zero coverage for the exact defect class design_notes exists to prevent. Replaced the command with exact-list equality: `mods[0].get('key_documents')==['ecosystem/README.md']`. Re-probed live (both variants, scratch-only, never touching the real NIZAM.json): the dangling-extra-path fixture and the existing-but-extra-path fixture (01_clean_state_preflight.md simulated present) BOTH now FAIL the tightened check -- closing the gap independent of whether C4 also happens to catch the dangling sub-case. FIX 2 (LOW, verification_index 5 in rev0 numbering -- now verification[5]): the version-bump check's acceptance_test TEXT claimed the version must be bumped 'PAST' 0.1.0 (directional) but the command only tested `!= '0.1.0'`, so a semver DOWNGRADE (e.g. 0.0.1, with a matching change_log entry) would incorrectly pass. Replaced with a genuine semver-tuple comparison (`(major,minor,patch) > (0,1,0)`, requiring the version to also match the strict MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH pattern). Re-probed live (in-memory fixture, 0.0.1 + change_log entry, never written to any repo file): now correctly FAILS. The anti-vacuity companion (verification[6]) was updated to run the identical tightened logic against HEAD's content (still correctly FAILS at HEAD, which is 0.1.0 with no change_log -- confirmed non-vacuous). FIX 3 (unsupported claim, non-blocking but addressed): scope.files_modify[0].purpose committed the README module-navigation table to a Shipped/Planned annotation per file's actual on-disk existence, but no verification entry checked for it. Re-read product_spec_005.md Sec 6/2.3 and the feature_list_005.json description: neither explicitly requires the annotation, but neither forbids it, and it is a genuine documentation-quality strengthening consistent with the spec's 'navigates the module's protocol documents' intent (F-002) -- chose to ADD coverage rather than delete the commitment. Added verification[9] (checks, for each of the 9 documents, that some line names the bare filename together with 'Shipped' or 'Planned' as determined DYNAMICALLY by testing the real filesystem at check time -- not a hardcoded list -- so it stays correct regardless of which of 033-036 have landed by generator time) and its anti-vacuity companion verification[10] (confirms HEAD's stub, which names none of the 9 documents, does not already satisfy it). All design_notes numeric cross-references to verification-array indices were re-derived and corrected against the final 19-entry array (insertions shifted every index from the old verification[9] onward by +2). Nothing outside these three findings' closures was changed: scope, non_goals, and every other verification entry's command are otherwise byte-identical to revision 0.

files_modify.purpose: Complete the existing 11-line stub: define the canonical 10-stage lifecycle (Bootstrap->Preflight->Baseline->Audit->Plan->Execute->Verify->Promote->Compare->Repeat) in prose, and add a module-navigation section listing all 9 numbered protocol documents (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md .. 08_ga_gate.md) by BARE filename (never directory-qualified as 'ecosystem/NN_....md') with a Shipped/Planned annotation per file's actual on-disk existence at authoring time. Bump frontmatter version past 0.1.0 with a matching change_log entry. authoritative_source, status, id, title, description keys remain otherwise unchanged (still 'ecosystem/README.md', 'active', 'nizam-ecosystem-module', etc.).

Append one new object to the top-level 'modules' array: {"path": "ecosystem", "purpose": <one-line summary of the module>, "key_documents": ["ecosystem/README.md"]}. No other array (capabilities/schemas/templates) is touched by this feature -- capability registration for the ecosystem lifecycle is handover F-010 / framework feature 040, explicitly out of scope here.

non_goals: No authoring of any of the 9 numbered protocol documents themselves (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md .. 08_ga_gate.md) -- those are features 033/034/035/036 (01/02/03/07) or explicitly deferred beyond phase 005 (00/04/05/06/08 per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3)

No edit to, or creation/deletion of, ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md -- that file is feature 033's sole deliverable, being implemented CONCURRENTLY in this same working tree by another agent; it is tolerated (allow-listed) in the scope guard as present/absent/in-flux, never authored or touched by this feature

No edit to any schema/*.json file (schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/engineering_finding.schema.json are features 037/038/039) or any file under tools/ (tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, tools/validate.sh changes are features 040/042) -- this feature performs zero schema/tooling work
No addition of a 'capabilities' array entry for the ecosystem module, and no edit to tools/skill.json or tools/SKILL.md -- capability routing is handover F-010 / framework feature 040

No edit to any file under standard/, methodology/, registry/, templates/, docs/architecture/, docs/guide/, CONTEXT.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, bootstrap.sh, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- this feature is fenced to exactly the two files in scope.files_modify

No edit to .agent/run_state.json under any circumstance -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned; the orchestrator batches state centrally across concurrently-dispatched features (032/033) and this feature must never write to it, even to advance current_feature or record completion

No edit to any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 032.json, and no edit to any historical .agent/qa/*.json -- those are immutable audit records. This EXPLICITLY includes tolerating (never touching) .agent/contracts/033.json and .agent/qa/033*.json/.agent/evidence/033*, which the concurrently-executing feature 033 legitimately creates/modifies in this same working tree

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md (root) -- unrelated residual artifact predating this feature, tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list only, not authorized for modification
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- committing is a separate, later pipeline action

verification.acceptance_test: F-002/AT (official, feature_list_005.json verbatim): ecosystem/README.md names the full canonical lifecycle (4-keyword form).
verification.command: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if all(k in t for k in ['preflight','baseline','audit','compare']) else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "README.md missing one or more of the 4 official lifecycle keywords"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: STRONGER (supersedes AT above per the full 10-stage lifecycle named in product_spec_005.md Sec 1 and the feature description): all 10 canonical stages present as whole-word matches, case-insensitive.
verification.command: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); stages=['bootstrap','preflight','baseline','audit','plan','execute','verify','promote','compare','repeat']; missing=[s for s in stages if not re.search(r'\b'+s+r'\b', t)]; sys.exit(1 if missing else 0)" && echo PASS || { echo "README.md missing one or more of the 10 canonical lifecycle stages"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY companion for the 10-stage check: confirm HEAD's ecosystem/README.md does NOT already satisfy it (proving the check is non-vacuous).
verification.command: ! (git show HEAD:ecosystem/README.md | python3 -c "import re,sys; t=sys.stdin.read().lower(); stages=['bootstrap','preflight','baseline','audit','plan','execute','verify','promote','compare','repeat']; missing=[s for s in stages if not re.search(r'\b'+s+r'\b', t)]; sys.exit(1 if missing else 0)") && echo PASS || { echo "HEAD ecosystem/README.md already names all 10 stages -- the check would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-002/AT (official, feature_list_005.json verbatim): ecosystem/README.md frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md frontmatter invalid"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (NDS consistency, not enforced by validate.sh's C8 since ecosystem/ is outside build_shipped_md_set -- see design_notes.c8_and_c7_do_not_apply): frontmatter version is bumped past the pre-existing stub's 0.1.0, with a matching change_log entry.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,re,sys; d=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); v=fm.get('version') or ''; m=re.match(r'^(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$', v); cl=fm.get('change_log') or []; ok = bool(m) and tuple(int(x) for x in m.groups()) > (0,1,0) and any(isinstance(e,dict) and e.get('version')==v for e in cl); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md frontmatter version is not a genuine semver increase past 0.1.0, or lacks a matching change_log entry"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY companion for the version-bump check: confirm HEAD's ecosystem/README.md frontmatter (version 0.1.0, no change_log) does NOT already satisfy the tightened semver-increase check (same logic as the check above, re-run against HEAD's content).
verification.command: ! (git show HEAD:ecosystem/README.md | python3 -c "import yaml,re,sys; d=sys.stdin.read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); v=fm.get('version') or ''; m=re.match(r'^(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$', v); cl=fm.get('change_log') or []; ok = bool(m) and tuple(int(x) for x in m.groups()) > (0,1,0) and any(isinstance(e,dict) and e.get('version')==v for e in cl); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)") && echo PASS || { echo "HEAD ecosystem/README.md already has a bumped version + change_log -- the check would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (module navigation, realizing 'navigates the module's protocol documents' from the feature description): all 9 numbered protocol documents (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md .. 08_ga_gate.md, per NIP-0001's 'Proposed Framework Surface') are named by bare filename.
verification.command: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); docs=['00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md','01_clean_state_preflight.md','02_evidence_baseline.md','03_engineering_audit.md','04_dependency_reconciliation.md','05_release_train_coordination.md','06_simplification_review.md','07_progress_comparison.md','08_ga_gate.md']; missing=[d for d in docs if d not in t]; import sys; sys.exit(1 if missing else 0)" && echo PASS || { echo "README.md module navigation missing one or more of the 9 protocol document references"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY companion for the module-navigation check: confirm HEAD's ecosystem/README.md (the 11-line stub) does NOT already name all 9 documents.
verification.command: ! (git show HEAD:ecosystem/README.md | python3 -c "import sys; t=sys.stdin.read().lower(); docs=['00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md','01_clean_state_preflight.md','02_evidence_baseline.md','03_engineering_audit.md','04_dependency_reconciliation.md','05_release_train_coordination.md','06_simplification_review.md','07_progress_comparison.md','08_ga_gate.md']; missing=[d for d in docs if d not in t]; sys.exit(1 if missing else 0)") && echo PASS || { echo "HEAD ecosystem/README.md already names all 9 protocol documents -- the check would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (closes the unsupported-claim gap per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json: scope.files_modify[0].purpose commits ecosystem/README.md's module-navigation table to a Shipped/Planned annotation per file's actual on-disk existence). For each of the 9 numbered protocol documents, some line of the body mentions that document's bare filename together with the word 'Shipped' if `ecosystem/<file>` exists on disk right now, or 'Planned' if it does not -- evaluated dynamically against the real filesystem at check time (not a hardcoded shipped/unshipped list), so it stays correct regardless of which of 033-036 have landed by the time this check runs.
ANTI-VACUITY companion for the Shipped/Planned annotation check: confirm HEAD's ecosystem/README.md (the 11-line stub, which names none of the 9 documents at all) does NOT already satisfy it.

REGRESSION GUARD (design_notes.design_constraint_no_dangling_references): every C9-shaped, directory-qualified path token (`[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_./-]*\.(md|json|sh|html|yml)`) found in ecosystem/README.md's BODY (frontmatter stripped) resolves on disk right now -- proves the module-navigation table never uses the 'ecosystem/NN_....md' directory-qualified form for a not-yet-shipped doc.
verification.command: python3 -c "import re,os,sys; t=open('ecosystem/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); parts=t.split('---'); body = '---'.join(parts[2:]) if len(parts)>=3 else t; tokens = re.findall(r'[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_./-]*\.(?:md|json|sh|html|yml)', body); bad=[tok for tok in tokens if not os.path.exists(tok.rstrip('.,;:'))]; sys.exit(1 if bad else 0)" && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md body contains a directory-qualified path reference that does not resolve on disk"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-002/AT (official, feature_list_005.json verbatim): NIZAM.json indexes the ecosystem module and every indexed path resolves (C4-green). KNOWN FALSE-PASS AT HEAD -- see design_notes.known_false_pass_in_official_acceptance_test. Kept for spec traceability; NOT the blocking gate (verification[10]/[10b] are).
verification.command: grep -q 'ecosystem' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0

verification.acceptance_test: STRONGER (the real gate, replacing the vacuous literal grep above): NIZAM.json's 'modules' array contains exactly one well-formed object with path=='ecosystem', a non-empty purpose, and key_documents EQUAL TO the exact single-element list ['ecosystem/README.md'] -- not merely containing it (per .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json required_fix #1: a membership-only test would pass a module entry that also lists a second, out-of-contract, possibly-dangling key_document such as 'ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md', which the incidental C4 backstop stops catching the moment feature 033 lands that path in this same concurrently-worked tree).
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); mods=[m for m in d.get('modules',[]) if m.get('path')=='ecosystem']; sys.exit(0 if (len(mods)==1 and mods[0].get('key_documents')==['ecosystem/README.md'] and mods[0].get('purpose')) else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json modules array missing a well-formed ecosystem module entry with key_documents EXACTLY ['ecosystem/README.md']"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY companion for the structural module-entry check: confirm HEAD's NIZAM.json modules array does NOT already contain a path=='ecosystem' entry.
verification.command: ! (git show HEAD:NIZAM.json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); mods=[m for m in d.get('modules',[]) if m.get('path')=='ecosystem']; sys.exit(0 if mods else 1)") && echo PASS || { echo "HEAD NIZAM.json already indexes an ecosystem module -- verification[10] would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-002/AT (official, feature_list_005.json verbatim): compliance validator stays green.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (scope guard, untracked-aware, per methodology/02 Sec 10 / tools/verify_lib.sh's vlib_scope_guard, which already excludes the entire .agent/ tree from its underlying `git status ... -- . ':(exclude).agent'` pathspec -- so .agent/contracts/033.json, .agent/qa/033*.json, and .agent/evidence/033* never need explicit allow-listing here): only ecosystem/README.md and NIZAM.json changed outside .agent/, tolerating the concurrently-authored ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (feature 033, never touched by this feature) and the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'ecosystem/README.md' 'NIZAM.json' 'ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, mirroring the precedent in contracts 025/028/031 exactly): no historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact was edited -- excludes only this feature's own new 032.json contract and 032*.json qa/review artifacts, PLUS the concurrently-executing feature 033's own 033.json contract and 033*.json qa/review artifacts (tolerated, never authored by this feature). .agent/run_state.json is deliberately NOT in the checked path set (orchestrator-owned, per non_goals).
verification.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/032.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/032*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/033.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/033*.json'); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo 'immutable audit record(s) touched:'; echo "$changed"; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): every schema/*.json file and every file under tools/ are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ tools/ && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/tooling file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ tools/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/DEBT.md is byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "docs/planning/DEBT.md was modified -- out of scope for this feature"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/032-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (.agent/evidence/032-verify-01.txt for the first entry .. .agent/evidence/032-verify-19.txt for the nineteenth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 19 entries as of revision 1). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
